proposals.title: SPEC Existence - Critical Coverage Gap
findings: Only 51.1% of public symbols have SPECs (137/268)
166 symbols lack documentation
Domain layer missing 10 critical SPECs
Components layer missing 40 SPECs
Utilities/constants missing 57 SPECs

priority_actions.action: Create SPECs for all domain entities and repositories
priority_actions.rationale: Domain layer is the foundation of Clean Architecture. Missing SPECs here violate constitution requirements for complete documentation of business logic.

priority_actions.action: Create SPECs for application hooks and infrastructure services
priority_actions.rationale: Application and infrastructure layers contain critical business logic and data access patterns that require documentation.
Component documentation is essential for UI maintenance and consistency. Focus on chart components first as they're core to the application.

priority_actions.action: Create SPECs for utility functions and constants
priority_actions.rationale: Utility functions are reused across the codebase. Documentation prevents misuse and ensures consistent behavior.

success_criteria: SPEC coverage reaches 75% (201/268 symbols)
All domain layer symbols have SPECs
All application layer symbols have SPECs
Critical infrastructure symbols have SPECs

proposals.title: I/O Strictness - Minor Improvements Needed
findings: Most SPECs have complete I/O specifications
14/15 points achieved (93.3%)
Minor gaps in some SPECs' preconditions/postconditions documentation

priority_actions.action: Review and enhance preconditions/postconditions for remaining 1 point
priority_actions.rationale: Achieving perfect score on I/O strictness ensures all behavior is documented.

success_criteria: All SPECs have complete input specifications
All SPECs have complete output specifications
All SPECs document preconditions
All SPECs document postconditions
All SPECs document invariants where applicable

proposals.title: Boundary Analysis - Insufficient Coverage
findings: Only 46.7% of required boundary value coverage achieved
Many SPECs have generic or incomplete boundary_values.yml
Missing boundary cases for edge conditions
Lack of systematic boundary value analysis

priority_actions.action: Enhance boundary_values.yml for all SPECs with actual boundaries
priority_actions.rationale: Boundary values are critical for preventing edge case bugs. Current generic templates don't provide value.

priority_actions.action: Perform boundary value analysis for each symbol type
methodology: For numeric types: min, max, min-1, max+1, zero, negative
For arrays: empty, single element, max capacity
For strings: empty, max length, special characters
For dates: min date, max date, leap year, timezone boundaries
For enums: all valid values, invalid values

success_criteria: All SPECs have specific boundary_values.yml (not generic templates)
Boundary cases cover min, max, and edge conditions
Each parameter type has appropriate boundary analysis
Boundary cases are testable and have corresponding tests

proposals.title: Error Coverage - Insufficient Error Scenarios
findings: Only 46.7% of required error coverage achieved
Many SPECs have empty or generic error_scenarios.yml
Missing error cases for external dependencies
Lack of systematic error scenario analysis

priority_actions.action: Create comprehensive error_scenarios.yml for all SPECs
targets: All database operations (connection failures, constraints, timeouts)
All validation functions (invalid types, out of range, malformed data)
All file operations (not found, permission denied, disk full)
priority_actions.rationale: Error scenarios are critical for resilience. Current gaps leave the system vulnerable to unhandled errors.

priority_actions.action: Map all error types to their scenarios
priority_actions.rationale: Systematic error type mapping ensures comprehensive error handling.

success_criteria: All SPECs have specific error_scenarios.yml (not generic templates)
Error scenarios cover all error types thrown by the symbol
Each error scenario includes expected behavior and error type
Error scenarios have corresponding tests

proposals.title: Test-SPEC Mapping - Critical Gap
findings: Only 6.7% of test-SPEC mapping achieved
189 test files exist but minimal test_matrix.yml files
Tests exist but aren't systematically mapped to SPECs
Missing traceability from tests to specifications

targets: Map existing test files to SPECs
Identify test gaps (SPEC requirements without tests)
priority_actions.rationale: Test-SPEC mapping is essential for ensuring tests validate specifications. Without it, we can't verify if tests cover requirements.

methodology: For each SPEC, list all boundary cases and error scenarios
Map each case to specific test files and test names
Identify SPEC requirements without corresponding tests
Create new tests to fill gaps
priority_actions.rationale: Systematic gap analysis ensures comprehensive test coverage.

priority_actions.action: Reorganize tests to align with SPEC structure
methodology: Name test files to match SPEC names
Add test comments referencing SPEC IDs
Create test suites for boundary cases and error scenarios
priority_actions.rationale: Aligned test structure improves maintainability and traceability.

success_criteria: All SPECs have test_matrix.yml files
All test files are mapped to SPECs
All boundary cases have corresponding tests
All error scenarios have corresponding tests
Test coverage for each SPEC is documented and measurable

proposals.title: Constitution Compliance - Minor Gap
findings: 70% constitution compliance achieved
Most SPECs document types and constraints
Some SPECs lack explicit validation documentation
Minor gaps in constraint specification

priority_actions.action: Ensure all SPECs document TypeScript types
targets: All input parameters must have explicit type documentation
All return values must have explicit type documentation
All type parameters must be documented with constraints
priority_actions.rationale: Type safety is a core constitution requirement. Explicit documentation prevents type-related bugs.
Explicit constraints ensure implementation matches specification.

targets: All external inputs must document validation
All Zod schemas must be referenced
All runtime validation must be documented
priority_actions.rationale: Validation is critical for preventing invalid data from entering the system.

success_criteria: All SPECs document TypeScript types for all inputs/outputs
All SPECs document constraints explicitly
All SPECs reference validation schemas where applicable
All SPECs comply with SYSTEM_CONSTITUTION.md requirements

proposals.title: Regression Design - Passing
findings: 80% regression design score achieved
Most SPECs have version information
Most SPECs have last updated timestamps
Minor improvements possible for changelog tracking

priority_actions.action: Ensure all SPECs have semantic version numbers
targets: All SPECs should follow semantic versioning (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH)
priority_actions.rationale: Consistent version tracking enables change management and regression detection.

success_criteria: All SPECs have version numbers
All SPECs have last updated timestamps
Version changes follow semantic versioning
Breaking changes are documented in SPEC changelogs

proposals.title: SPEC Freshness - Stale Documentation
findings: 60% freshness score achieved
Many SPECs haven't been updated in > 7 days
Some SPECs may be out of sync with implementation
Lack of regular SPEC update process

methodology: Review and update SPECs when code changes
Update SPECs as part of code review process
Require SPEC updates for PR approval
priority_actions.rationale: Fresh SPECs ensure documentation stays synchronized with implementation.

priority_actions.action: Review and update all SPECs older than 30 days
targets: Identify SPECs with last update > 30 days ago
Compare SPEC content with current implementation
Update SPECs to match current behavior
Add missing boundary cases and error scenarios
priority_actions.rationale: Bulk update brings all SPECs to current state.

priority_actions.action: Add SPEC freshness to quality monitoring
implementation: Add SPEC age check to meta_checker.py
Alert when SPECs are > 30 days old
priority_actions.rationale: Automated monitoring ensures SPECs don't become stale.

success_criteria: All SPECs updated within last 30 days
SPEC updates are part of development workflow
SPEC freshness is monitored and reported
Stale SPECs trigger alerts for review

phase_1_critical.action: Create missing SPECs for domain layer
Create test_matrix.yml for all existing SPECs

phase_3_medium.action: Update stale SPECs and implement freshness monitoring
Create missing SPECs for components and utilities

recommended_approach.strategy: Incremental improvement with continuous audit cycles
recommended_approach.sprint_1: Focus on S-001 (Domain SPECs) + S-005 (Test matrices) - 14-18 hours
recommended_approach.sprint_4: Polish S-002 + S-007 + remaining S-001 - 8-12 hours
recommended_approach.target_achievement: Reach 80+ points with all axes passing thresholds
